priority.when: Use first to understand the widget directory and discover comparable examples
priority.why: Project structure should guide widget creation and modification decisions

priority.when: Use after locating candidate widgets or metadata files
priority.why: Reading real examples prevents inconsistent widget generation

priority.when: Use for precise edits to existing widget files
priority.why: Targeted edits preserve surrounding implementation details

priority.when: Use for new widget files or full file rewrites only when intentional
priority.why: New files and complete rewrites require explicit and complete content

priority.when: Use for scaffold generation or project-local validation commands
priority.why: Commands are useful for repeatable scaffolding and lightweight checks

best_practices.practice: Inspect widgets/ before creating a new widget
Use surrounding widgets as reference points for naming and support files
Read comparable widget files together whenever possible
Compare structure, metadata, preview assets, and documentation before editing
Prefer apply_diff for surgical changes to existing widget files
Read the current file first to match exact content and indentation
Use for creating new files in widgets/ or for intentional full rewrites
Write complete file contents and do not omit unchanged sections in rewrites
Use local commands such as scaffold generation only when they support the widget-authoring workflow
Do not use packaging commands in this mode

boundary.rule: Do not package widgets into TWX artifacts in this mode
When the task shifts to packaging or deployment, hand off to the package manager mode
